Motion Contesting Jason Frosts Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ALLANA, BUICK & BERS, INC's Motion Contesting Jason Frosts Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Allana, Buck & Bers' "motion contesting Jason Frost's application for good faith settlement determination" is denied. Frost was a minor player in this construction defect dispute. He was paid $13,600 for preparing a "settlement sufficiency analysis." (Oliveira Dec. 3:17-18.) He settled with plaintiff for $50,000 in a negotiation supervised by a respected mediator. (Id. at 3:20-22.) The settlement's good faith is further supported by declarations from plaintiff's and Frost's counsel. As is typical, Frost's settlement is attacked by a remaining defendant who relies largely on plaintiff's inflated claim as a denominator. But the settlement is not "so far 'out of the ballpark' as to be inconsistent with the equitable objectives" of the good faith statutes. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde Associates (1985) 38 Cal. 3d 488, 499-500.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported.(302/RBU)
